title: What cloud providers does CloudDash support
content: CloudDash natively supports Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and Microsoft Azure. For AWS, we integrate via CloudWatch and CloudTrail. For GCP, we use Google Cloud Operations suite (formerly Stackdriver). For Azure, we pull metrics directly from Azure Monitor. Setting up a new provider takes less than 5 minutes using our 1-click IAM role provisioner. Each provider dashboard gives you a unified view of your instances, databases, and network usage. Multi-cloud cost comparison is available on the Pro and Enterprise tiers. This section provides additional context to ensure comprehensive documentation. The CloudDash platform is built with robustness and scalability in mind. When encountering issues, always verify your configuration settings and ensure that your network allows communication with our endpoints. If you continue to experience difficulties, please consult the troubleshooting steps outlined previously. Our support team is available to assist you with any advanced configurations or unexpected behavior. Remember to keep your agent versions up to date to benefit from the latest features and security patches. Furthermore, monitoring your usage limits can prevent unexpected service interruptions. For detailed API specifications, refer to our developer portal. We are committed to providing reliable service and continuous improvements to our platform. Should you have any feedback or feature requests, do not hesitate to reach out. Thank you for choosing CloudDash as your monitoring solution. We hope this guide has been helpful in resolving your query.
